ogTitle: Stanwell Park vs State Mine Gully: Climate Safety

heading: Stanwell Park vs State Mine Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stanwell Park, New South Wales and State Mine Gully, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: State Mine Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Stanwell Park (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stanwell Park or State Mine Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stanwell Park has a flood risk score of 37/100, while State Mine Gully scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stanwell Park scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. State Mine Gully scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stanwell Park or State Mine Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. State Mine Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
